title: Legatura Long-Term Plan
northStar: Make local 1+n agent software delivery trustworthy by compiling bounded work from durable project truth, keeping execution replaceable, and accepting only exact changes supported by discriminating evidence and explicit authority.

coreCompletion.definition: Legatura's core is complete only when the reference 1+3 scenario proves bounded context, capability isolation, deterministic recovery, conflict-aware integration, and exact acceptance without relying on worker transcripts or worker self-ratification.

bootstrapBaseline.head: 6413924bb66fe3cc1aeb460b2592f481e1a53f02
bootstrapBaseline.rationale: These capabilities existed and passed the repository's exact-binding and lifecycle suites before the Development Plan became machine authority.
bootstrapBaseline.residualUncertainty: The bootstrap predates evidence-bound Outcome Transition records and must not be used as precedent for completing post-bootstrap Outcomes.

principles: Outcomes describe durable capabilities or closed uncertainty, never files, tickets, or implementation tasks.
A plan amendment is accepted before an implementation Change may use the amended plan; a Change cannot authorize itself against newer governance.
Stage exit is decided from exact Evidence against exit criteria, never from coverage percentages, task counts, or dates.

A later-discovered prerequisite never rewrites active Outcome Criteria or an existing Closure Contract; canonical append-only blocksGapClosureRefs and exact blocksOutcomeTransitionRoutes on its own open Knowledge Gap become compiler-derived transitive Gap dispositions that prevent only the named Gap closures and Outcome routes until each baseline-open blocker satisfies its own governed Closure Contract atomically inside the target Transition.
The master consumes structured state and content-addressed artifacts; full worker conversations are not coordination state.

Batch semantic projections compile one compiler-authenticated Project Model Provider covering every Contract Claim for the stable current Model and pass that exact process-local Provider through validation and Profile compilation, then union the query's actually referenced non-builtin source Claims and compile at most one Provider for each unique verified frozen Governance Baseline content; scalar Interfaces and adapters project from those Providers instead of recompiling policy per Claim, record, or view.

Compiler-owned resource observations expose requested-Claim normalization work, emitted route count, and total route bytes so a consuming query can charge each unique compilation once under one aggregate ceiling; cache reuse is not recharged, call-local limits cannot reset that ceiling, and these operational facts are never assurance scores, confidence, readiness, or Evidence of correctness.
Workers execute bounded work and report observations; they do not define sufficiency, waive obligations, or accept their own output.
Minimum Gates protect routine Change economics; Full Gates protect composition, integration, and release.

Architecture and Workbench projections compile from stable, pre-bounded source snapshots; Project Model alone compiles canonical Module Claim visibility and acceptance-eligible original Gate and command routes, Change Kernel owns stable source orchestration plus complete authoring and lifecycle-action projections with a closed disabled-reason vocabulary, and CLI, HTTP, and browser adapters preserve those identifiers and digests without omission, duplication, invention, semantic recompilation, or collapse into scores, percentages, health, readiness, confidence, or green lights.

Architecture Profile history queries select one canonical hard-bounded Change window inside Change Kernel before Profile facts or historical route products compile; process-local authenticated continuations traverse only one unchanged stabilized snapshot, and CLI, HTTP, and browser adapters project each Kernel window and opaque continuation without decoding, reordering, cross-snapshot coalescing, or unbounded accumulation.

Command execution truth is one bounded raw-byte observation under an explicit support profile: Assurance Runtime owns timeout, cancellation, stream capture, and confirmed settlement, while Change Kernel consumes the exact terminal facts and never turns unsupported, malformed, controlled, signaled, or unconfirmed observations into positive Gate Evidence.
New architecture should deepen the Worker Execution and Coordinator Modules instead of multiplying shallow framework Modules.

referenceAcceptanceScenario.topology: One persistent master coordinates three replaceable local workers in an incrementally adopted brownfield repository whose app and apk Modules are independent and whose relay Module consumes both public Contracts.
faults: One worker terminates after producing a partial report.
Two workers attempt conflicting writes or incompatible Contract changes.
mustDemonstrate: The brownfield repository is adopted without treating unmodeled areas as governed or requiring whole-repository understanding.
Each Work Unit has one primary Module, a bounded Context Capsule, and a capability-bound workspace lease.
The master recovers from durable structured state without replaying worker transcripts.
Top-level coordination state contains no transcript, log, or source bodies and grows independently of referenced artifact payload bytes.
Independent work proceeds concurrently while path and Contract conflicts are stopped before composition.
A worker loss cannot produce Accepted state and retry does not duplicate acceptance.
The composed Accepted Change Packages pass one Full Gate against the exact integration content.

outcomes.outcome: A repository can declare and validate its Project Model, Modules, Contracts, Gates, authorities, Assurance Boundary, and Knowledge Gaps.

exitCriteria: Dangling Module, Contract, Claim, Gate, authority, and assurance references fail closed.
The validated model and its source files have a canonical digest.

nonGoals: Automatic worker execution
A general-purpose architecture graph database

outcomes.outcome: A Change has a governed lifecycle whose Accepted Change Package binds intent, baseline, ChangeSet, Evidence, closure, authority, and exact repository content.

exitCriteria: A worker observation cannot accept its own Change.
Stale or altered repository content invalidates acceptance.
Model amendments require durable Knowledge Closure.

nonGoals: Authenticated human identity
Remote attestation

outcomes.outcome: Routine Changes use Module-focused Minimum Gates while integration uses a Full Gate against the sealed acceptance subject.

exitCriteria: A Minimum Gate selects only commands declared for the primary Module.
Integration requires current Full-Gate assurance when policy declares it.
Test count and coverage percentage are not used as trust claims.

nonGoals: Running every test for every edit
Claim-level test pruning before it proves necessary

outcomes.outcome: Users can inspect the Project Model and govern Changes through an installed CLI and a bounded local Workbench.

exitCriteria: The packaged CLI entrypoint executes after local installation.
The HTTP interface is loopback-bound and delegates lifecycle facts to the Kernel.
Browser semantics remain explicitly provisional until independently exercised.

nonGoals: Hosted collaboration service
Replacing an IDE

outcomes.outcome: Every ordinary Change is compiled against an active Development Outcome through an explicit, reviewable contribution, and humans can inspect how architecture Claims map to Gates, Evidence, and open Gaps.

exitCriteria: The Development Plan is validated, included in the frozen Governance Baseline, and exposed by Project Model inspection.
Compilation rejects missing, unknown, or non-active Outcome references when plan alignment is required.
Only selected Outcomes enter the Context Capsule and acceptance subject.
Each ordinary Change binds its primary Module and Claims to a reviewable contribution toward the selected Outcome, with any exception decided by Plan authority.
Outcome activation and achievement transitions bind exact Evidence and authority to their declared transition and exit criteria.
Project Atlas projects Outcome to Claim, Gate, current Evidence, Residual Uncertainty, and Knowledge Gap from the same fact authorities.

criteria.statement: The Development Plan is validated, included in the frozen Governance Baseline, and exposed by Project Model inspection.
Compilation rejects missing, unknown, or non-active Outcome references when plan alignment is required.
Only selected Outcomes enter the Context Capsule and acceptance subject.
Each ordinary Change binds its primary Module and Claims to a reviewable contribution toward the selected Outcome, with any exception decided by Plan authority.
Outcome activation and achievement transitions bind exact Evidence and authority to their declared transition and exit criteria.
Project Atlas projects Outcome to Claim, Gate, current Evidence, Residual Uncertainty, and Knowledge Gap from the same fact authorities.

nonGoals: Issue tracker synchronization
Automatic Outcome completion
A generic portfolio planning tool
Independently exercising browser rendering; that belongs to LGT-014

outcomes.outcome: Project Model compiles every tracked repository path to exactly one Module write owner or one explicit ungoverned disposition, rejects latent and concrete ambiguous ownership, and Change Kernel binds Context Capsule plus touched or prospective path enforcement to that exact result.

exitCriteria: Given one bounded exact tracked-path observation and the frozen exact-or-recursive-prefix policy, Project Model rejects every tracked path with no Module write owner or explicit ungoverned disposition and every latent or concrete owner-owner, owner-disposition, or disposition-disposition overlap without precedence.

Context Capsule write scope and Change Kernel touched or prospective path enforcement consume the same opaque ownership product from one stabilized exact Model and tracked-path observation, and fail closed when that product is absent, forged, stale, disposition-only, over limit, or drifts from the Candidate's frozen ownership.

criteria.statement: Given one bounded exact tracked-path observation and the frozen exact-or-recursive-prefix policy, Project Model rejects every tracked path with no Module write owner or explicit ungoverned disposition and every latent or concrete owner-owner, owner-disposition, or disposition-disposition overlap without precedence.

Context Capsule write scope and Change Kernel touched or prospective path enforcement consume the same opaque ownership product from one stabilized exact Model and tracked-path observation, and fail closed when that product is absent, forged, stale, disposition-only, over limit, or drifts from the Candidate's frozen ownership.

nonGoals: Inferring ownership from commit history, file contents, imports, or naming conventions.
Automatically repairing ownership conflicts or rewriting Module path declarations.
Making read context exclusive or treating ungoverned dispositions as write authority or assurance.

outcomes.outcome: Project Model schemas have explicit compatibility, migration, and refusal semantics across Legatura upgrades.

exitCriteria: Unknown incompatible schema versions fail closed.
Supported migrations preserve normative meaning and produce reviewable amendments.

nonGoals: Supporting every historical prerelease schema forever

outcomes.outcome: A declared local direct-process support profile produces bounded terminal-state-complete Command Observations, and the Kernel fails closed on unsupported, controlled, malformed, or unconfirmed outcomes.

exitCriteria: The declared local direct-process profile yields one bounded raw-byte Command Observation that keeps support, control trigger, confirmed close, actual exit or signal, and per-stream capture, length, digest, encoding, and truncation facts orthogonal for natural exit, signal, timeout, cancellation, start failure, and output pressure; unsupported profiles, malformed adapter reports, controlled termination, or unconfirmed quiescence cannot become positive Gate Evidence.

criteria.statement: The declared local direct-process profile yields one bounded raw-byte Command Observation that keeps support, control trigger, confirmed close, actual exit or signal, and per-stream capture, length, digest, encoding, and truncation facts orthogonal for natural exit, signal, timeout, cancellation, start failure, and output pressure; unsupported profiles, malformed adapter reports, controlled termination, or unconfirmed quiescence cannot become positive Gate Evidence.

nonGoals: A distributed job runner
OS sandboxing or preventing descendants that deliberately escape the observed process group
Shell-command portability or Windows support without a separate conformance profile
Treating captured text as authoritative instead of raw bytes

outcomes.outcome: Architecture Acceptance Profiles remain inspectable as accepted history grows by traversing authenticated, snapshot-bound, hard-bounded windows whose exact Kernel semantics are only projected by CLI, loopback HTTP, and browser adapters.

exitCriteria: Every Profile request compiles at most one canonical Change window from one stabilized composite snapshot below fixed record, fact, unique-baseline route-work, route-byte, and output-byte ceilings; successive Kernel-issued continuations over the unchanged snapshot visit every Change exactly once, and malformed, modified, unissued, stale, cross-snapshot, or cross-query continuations fail closed.

CLI, loopback HTTP, and one DOM browser workflow preserve exact per-window source bindings, facts, relations, bounds, digests, and opaque continuations while traversing all windows without omission, duplication, cross-snapshot coalescing, cursor decoding, governance recomputation, aggregate assurance, or unbounded presentation state.

criteria.statement: Every Profile request compiles at most one canonical Change window from one stabilized composite snapshot below fixed record, fact, unique-baseline route-work, route-byte, and output-byte ceilings; successive Kernel-issued continuations over the unchanged snapshot visit every Change exactly once, and malformed, modified, unissued, stale, cross-snapshot, or cross-query continuations fail closed.

CLI, loopback HTTP, and one DOM browser workflow preserve exact per-window source bindings, facts, relations, bounds, digests, and opaque continuations while traversing all windows without omission, duplication, cross-snapshot coalescing, cursor decoding, governance recomputation, aggregate assurance, or unbounded presentation state.

nonGoals: Raising the whole-Profile 2 MiB or aggregate route-query ceiling to fit current history
Returning all accepted history in one payload or retaining unbounded browser or CLI state
Making continuations or ignored local Accepted history portable across Kernel processes, restarts, clones, or multi-writer workspaces
Pixel-perfect visual regression testing

outcomes.outcome: Minimum verification is pruned by exact Claim obligations only when Module-focused selection is no longer the smallest high-confidence route.

exitCriteria: A measured workload demonstrates material avoidable cost in the Module-focused route.
Claim-sensitive selection cannot omit an Oracle required by the affected Contract delta.

nonGoals: Reducing command count as an end in itself

outcomes.outcome: A versioned app/apk/relay brownfield reference proves incremental adoption through existing Legatura interfaces: relay is governed against public app and apk Contracts, provisional, opaque, and ungoverned areas remain explicit, and one relay Change reaches an Accepted Package without whole-repository modeling.

exitCriteria: The versioned app/apk/relay reference materializes as a valid Project Model in which relay is governed, app and apk are independent provisional public-Contract providers, legacy internals are opaque, and every tracked path is assigned exactly once to a Module owner or an explicit non-authorizing ungoverned disposition.

Using the unchanged Change Kernel lifecycle, one ordinary relay Change compiles a Context Capsule that exposes dependency Contracts but no app, apk, or legacy implementation path, then reaches an exact Accepted Change Package without closing or concealing the reference repository's open Knowledge Gaps.

The existing CLI inspection path projects the materialized reference's exact Module assurance states, public Contract Claims, Gate routes, and open Knowledge Gaps without a special adoption command or an inferred completeness conclusion.

criteria.statement: The versioned app/apk/relay reference materializes as a valid Project Model in which relay is governed, app and apk are independent provisional public-Contract providers, legacy internals are opaque, and every tracked path is assigned exactly once to a Module owner or an explicit non-authorizing ungoverned disposition.

Using the unchanged Change Kernel lifecycle, one ordinary relay Change compiles a Context Capsule that exposes dependency Contracts but no app, apk, or legacy implementation path, then reaches an exact Accepted Change Package without closing or concealing the reference repository's open Knowledge Gaps.

The existing CLI inspection path projects the materialized reference's exact Module assurance states, public Contract Claims, Gate routes, and open Knowledge Gaps without a special adoption command or an inferred completeness conclusion.

nonGoals: Worker Execution Interface, Work Specification, Worker Report, Execution Record, capability, or provider semantics; those belong to LGT-020
Context Capsule materialization, filesystem read enforcement, or Context Expansion recording and impact recomputation; those belong to LGT-021
Automatically inferring complete architecture or ownership for unknown code
Adding a legatura adopt command or browser onboarding flow
Governing app, apk, or legacy implementation internals as part of the relay adoption proof

outcomes.outcome: A planned or conditional Outcome can be refined before activation through append-only Plan-authority history, while activation freezes its statement and stable Criteria and terminal history remains immutable.

exitCriteria: Every pre-activation semantic revision records prior and current digests, rationale, and Plan authority without deleting the earlier meaning.
Activating an Outcome freezes its statement and stable Criteria; active, achieved, and retired semantics cannot be rewritten.
A revision cannot authorize implementation against the amended Plan within the same Change.

nonGoals: Rewriting active or terminal Outcomes
Treating task reprioritization as Outcome refinement

outcomes.outcome: A planned or conditional Outcome can be revised before activation through an append-only Plan-authority ledger, while activation freezes its complete normalized definition and active or terminal history remains immutable.

exitCriteria: Every pre-activation semantic revision records prior and current digests, rationale, and Plan authority without deleting the earlier meaning.
Activating an Outcome freezes its statement and stable Criteria; active, achieved, and retired semantics cannot be rewritten.
An Amendment that appends a Revision contains no Outcome status Transition, and a revision cannot authorize implementation against the amended Plan within the same Change.

criteria.statement: Every pre-activation semantic revision records prior and current digests, rationale, and Plan authority without deleting the earlier meaning.
Activating an Outcome freezes its statement and stable Criteria; active, achieved, and retired semantics cannot be rewritten.
An Amendment that appends a Revision contains no Outcome status Transition, and a revision cannot authorize implementation against the amended Plan within the same Change.

nonGoals: Rewriting active, achieved, or retired Outcomes
Treating priority, assignment, deadlines, or task progress as Outcome revisions
Combining any Outcome Revision with any Outcome status Transition in one Amendment
A generic event bus or Project Model migration framework

outcomes.outcome: Every Local Workbench authoring and acceptance input is a bounded, source-bound Kernel projection, while Profile traversal fails completely and canonically at process and browser boundaries without reconstructing governance semantics.

exitCriteria: One stabilized Workbench query projects the complete selectable Plan Outcome options and exact per-Change Knowledge Closure and authority-decision input requirements alongside Claims, routes, and lifecycle actions; every requirement is bounded and source-bound, and no adapter filters raw Plan facts or invents acceptance policy.

CLI, loopback HTTP, and browser adapters preserve the Kernel requirement projection and remain failure-complete: JSON inspection stays syntactically valid on a later-window failure, an initial browser refresh cannot retain a prior page as current, and a successor failure preserves the last valid page with a structured bounded error.

Profile windows use one explicit runtime-independent Change-id ordering and reject continuations issued in the future as well as malformed, modified, expired, unissued, cross-process, cross-snapshot, and cross-query continuations.

criteria.statement: One stabilized Workbench query projects the complete selectable Plan Outcome options and exact per-Change Knowledge Closure and authority-decision input requirements alongside Claims, routes, and lifecycle actions; every requirement is bounded and source-bound, and no adapter filters raw Plan facts or invents acceptance policy.

CLI, loopback HTTP, and browser adapters preserve the Kernel requirement projection and remain failure-complete: JSON inspection stays syntactically valid on a later-window failure, an initial browser refresh cannot retain a prior page as current, and a successor failure preserves the last valid page with a structured bounded error.

Profile windows use one explicit runtime-independent Change-id ordering and reject continuations issued in the future as well as malformed, modified, expired, unissued, cross-process, cross-snapshot, and cross-query continuations.

nonGoals: Turning browser forms into a second policy engine
Portable continuations or portable Accepted history
Adding aggregate assurance, confidence, score, percentage, health, readiness, or green-light conclusions
Claim-sensitive Minimum Gate pruning; that remains conditional LGT-015 work

outcomes.outcome: Workbench creation and Architecture Profile surfaces consume content-verified Kernel projections whose final Claim eligibility and orthogonal assurance facts are never reconstructed or collapsed in the browser.

exitCriteria: For every supported Change kind, exact selected Plan Outcome, and primary Module, Change Kernel emits the final bounded selectable Claim options and ordered closed disabled reasons from the same compiler-owned Plan and Claim-route semantics that enforce Change creation; the browser performs only exact projection lookup.

Before any Workbench or Architecture Profile projection becomes browser state or DOM input, the browser verifies its canonical content digest and rejects stale semantic mutation, aggregate assurance injection, malformed selection routes, and vocabulary drift while preserving the same-origin loopback trust boundary.

criteria.statement: For every supported Change kind, exact selected Plan Outcome, and primary Module, Change Kernel emits the final bounded selectable Claim options and ordered closed disabled reasons from the same compiler-owned Plan and Claim-route semantics that enforce Change creation; the browser performs only exact projection lookup.

Before any Workbench or Architecture Profile projection becomes browser state or DOM input, the browser verifies its canonical content digest and rejects stale semantic mutation, aggregate assurance injection, malformed selection routes, and vocabulary drift while preserving the same-origin loopback trust boundary.

nonGoals: Treating a SHA-256 content digest as remote-server authentication or a signature
Adding aggregate assurance, confidence, score, percentage, health, readiness, or green-light conclusions
Moving Plan, Contract, Gate, lifecycle, or acceptance policy into browser code
Expanding the Workbench beyond the declared loopback boundary

outcomes.outcome: A deep Worker Execution Interface defines Work Specification, Context Expansion Request, Worker Report, and Execution Record without granting acceptance authority.

exitCriteria: Worker inputs, capabilities, expansions, observations, and outputs are durably represented.
A deterministic conformance harness rejects lifecycle, capability, expansion, or report semantics that violate the Interface.
Provider-specific details are absent from the Kernel-facing Work Specification, Worker Report, and Execution Record.

nonGoals: Worker scheduling
Provider-specific transcript storage
Proving adapter independence; that belongs to LGT-023

outcomes.outcome: A real worker consumes a materialized Context Capsule and every extra read becomes a recorded Context Expansion with recomputed impact.

exitCriteria: Initial context is bounded and independently reproducible.
Dependency implementation paths are absent from initial context; reading them requires a recorded expansion that recomputes impact.
Out-of-capsule reads cannot become silent coordination knowledge.

nonGoals: Sending the whole repository to every worker

outcomes.outcome: Each worker receives an isolated workspace lease and an explicit capability profile that blocks out-of-scope writes and distinguishes observed from enforced controls.

exitCriteria: At least one declared local execution profile independently proves that an out-of-scope write is blocked at the execution boundary.
Observed-only profiles are labeled as such and cannot satisfy an enforced capability Claim.
The system never describes same-user process conventions as an OS sandbox.

nonGoals: Universal container orchestration

outcomes.outcome: The Worker Execution Interface has one deterministic scripted adapter and one real agent adapter with identical trust semantics.

exitCriteria: The deterministic scripted adapter and real agent adapter both pass the LGT-020 conformance harness.
Adapter failure, timeout, truncation, or disappearance fails closed.
Provider-specific details do not leak into the Kernel acceptance model.

nonGoals: An agent marketplace
Supporting many providers before the seam is proven

outcomes.outcome: One real bounded worker can complete intent-to-acceptance locally through the existing Kernel without acquiring decision authority.

exitCriteria: A worker crash cannot create Accepted state.
The Execution Record explains supplied context and capabilities plus produced artifacts.
Human authority accepts the exact Kernel package, not an agent summary.

nonGoals: Master coordination
Parallel execution

outcomes.outcome: A multi-Module intent deterministically compiles into a dependency-aware Work Graph whose Work Units each have one primary Module.

exitCriteria: Equivalent governed inputs produce the same Work Graph semantics.
Contract dependencies and blocked prerequisites are explicit before execution.

nonGoals: LLM-authored decomposition as an unreviewed fact authority

outcomes.outcome: An Artifact Ledger stores content-addressed worker detail while structured Worker Reports expose only decisions, deltas, blockers, risks, and drill-down references.

exitCriteria: Coordinator state contains no transcript, log, or source bodies and its serialized size is independent of referenced artifact payload bytes.
Every summarized fact can be traced to an immutable detailed artifact.

nonGoals: Using chat transcripts as the durable event model

outcomes.outcome: A persistent Coordinator state machine dispatches ready Work Units from governed dependencies, capabilities, Evidence state, and Knowledge Gaps without becoming an Oracle or Decision Authority.

exitCriteria: Scheduling reasons are reconstructed from durable structured facts.
Coordinator policy cannot waive Evidence or accept worker output.

nonGoals: Autonomous product prioritization

outcomes.outcome: A bounded Coordination Snapshot gives humans and the master the same view of goals, graph state, Contract deltas, Evidence gaps, conflicts, and pending decisions.

exitCriteria: For ready, blocked, conflicting, and decision-pending reference states, the top-level snapshot identifies the next dispatch or exact escalation without artifact expansion.
Detailed source, logs, and conversations are loaded only by referenced drill-down.

nonGoals: A general observability dashboard

outcomes.outcome: Coordinator replay is deterministic and idempotent enough to recover after restart without transcript replay, duplicate dispatch, or duplicate acceptance.

exitCriteria: Restart resumes the same ready, running, blocked, and done semantics.
Repeated reports and commands do not fork accepted state.

nonGoals: Geo-replicated consensus

outcomes.outcome: Independent Work Units run concurrently in isolated local workspaces with capability-bound leases.

exitCriteria: Disjoint Module work proceeds concurrently without sharing mutable workspaces.
Lease cancellation and expiry stop further authorized mutation.

nonGoals: Remote worker fleets

outcomes.outcome: Composition detects path overlap, Contract incompatibility, and downstream baseline invalidation before merge.

exitCriteria: Conflicting writes cannot silently compose.
Accepted Contract amendments force dependent work to recompile against the new baseline.

nonGoals: Automatically resolving semantic conflicts

outcomes.outcome: An integration queue composes compatible Accepted Change Packages and binds the result to one exact Full-Gate subject.

exitCriteria: Only compatible sealed packages enter composition.
The final Full Gate runs once against the exact composed content before integration.

nonGoals: Replacing Git hosting pull requests

outcomes.outcome: Cancellation, retry, idempotency, and worker-loss semantics prevent duplicate effects or ambiguous terminal states.

exitCriteria: A lost worker leaves explicit recoverable state.
Duplicate reports, retries, and late arrivals cannot duplicate acceptance or integration.

nonGoals: Infinite automatic retry

outcomes.outcome: The local 1+3 reference scenario proves the complete trusted collaboration architecture and defines core product completion.

exitCriteria: All reference scenario mustDemonstrate statements are supported by exact Evidence.
The app, apk, and relay reference repository completes independent work concurrently and rejects one incompatible Contract composition.
Removing worker transcripts does not prevent recovery, coordination explanation, or acceptance audit.
No worker or Coordinator can self-ratify.

nonGoals: Cloud multi-tenancy
Crashbench as a core dependency

outcomes.outcome: Authority identities and decisions can be authenticated and cryptographically bound when multi-user trust becomes a real requirement.

exitCriteria: A concrete multi-user threat model exists before activation.
Forged local identity assertions are rejected.

nonGoals: Building identity infrastructure before a consumer exists

outcomes.outcome: Remote execution has authenticated transport, result attestation, and explicit trust boundaries.

exitCriteria: A concrete remote-worker use case and threat model exist before activation.
Unattested remote observations cannot become trusted Evidence.

nonGoals: Generic cloud compute orchestration

outcomes.outcome: Network, secret, and external-tool capabilities are policy-bound and auditable for remote or privileged workers.

exitCriteria: Capabilities default closed and are lease-scoped.
Secret and network use is visible in the Execution Record without exposing secret values.

nonGoals: A universal secrets manager

outcomes.outcome: A durable multi-user daemon enforces project authority and coordination policy across authenticated users.

exitCriteria: A concrete concurrent multi-user workflow exists before activation.
Restart and concurrent decisions preserve one authoritative state history.

nonGoals: General SaaS project management

outcomes.outcome: The achieved trusted core remains usable and its security, data, acceptance, exact-binding, and fail-closed invariants do not regress while the roadmap advances.

exitCriteria: The Change binds the violated achieved Claim to complete failed Evidence and contains the smallest durable repair.
Existing Evidence and authority requirements remain in force; urgency grants no waiver.
Any structural follow-up becomes a normal planned Outcome before feature work resumes.

nonGoals: Feature delivery
Convenience refactoring
Deadline-driven reprioritization

previousDefinition.outcome: A deep Worker Execution Interface defines Work Specification, Context Expansion Request, Worker Report, and Execution Record without granting acceptance authority.

exitCriteria: A deterministic conformance harness rejects lifecycle, capability, expansion, or report semantics that violate the Interface.
Provider-specific details are absent from the Kernel-facing Work Specification, Worker Report, and Execution Record.
Worker inputs, capabilities, expansions, observations, and outputs are durably represented.

nonGoals: Provider-specific transcript storage
Proving adapter independence; that belongs to LGT-023
Worker scheduling

outcomeRevisions.rationale: Replace retired incomplete predecessor LGT-017 with its achieved append-only revision successor LGT-018 before Worker Execution planning begins.

previousDefinition.outcome: Every governed tracked path has one explicit Module owner or an explicit ungoverned disposition, and overlapping write ownership is rejected.

exitCriteria: Generated Context Capsules cannot silently inherit ambiguous paths.
Unknown ownership and overlapping writable ownership fail model validation.

nonGoals: Inferring ownership from commit history

outcomeRevisions.rationale: Replace the speculative path-ownership statement with stable exact Criteria, direct Fact and Module Claims, a fixed attack proof, and an explicit Context Capsule binding before the Outcome is activated.

previousDefinition.outcome: Human-facing projections preserve Kernel and Project Model semantics through one independently exercised high-value workflow.

exitCriteria: A real DOM workflow proves the displayed architecture and Change facts match the underlying APIs.
No snapshot-test wall is introduced.

nonGoals: Owning or recomputing Project Model facts instead of rendering their authoritative projection
Pixel-perfect visual regression testing

outcomeRevisions.rationale: Replace LGT-014's broad projection wording and already-closed legacy Profile Gap with stable window-traversal Criteria, the existing snapshot and assurance-dimension Claims, and explicit open traversal and DOM Gaps before activation; Module-owned window Contracts and fixed Closure routes must be declared in later Changes and this Revision grants neither implementation authority nor a lifecycle transition.

previousDefinition.outcome: Assurance Runtime support boundaries for timeout, encoding, truncation, cancellation, and platforms are explicit and fail closed.

exitCriteria: Every supported process outcome is represented without losing exit, signal, timeout, stream, or truncation state.
Unsupported environments are reported as unsupported rather than treated as assured.

nonGoals: A distributed job runner

outcomeRevisions.rationale: Replace the legacy exit list with one stable Criterion that binds the existing Assurance Runtime and Change Kernel Claims to an explicit local direct-process profile and the open subprocess boundary before activation; later owner-scoped Changes must declare proof Claims, fixed routes, implementation, and Kernel adoption.

previousDefinition.outcome: A brownfield repository can adopt Legatura incrementally, leaving unknown areas provisional or opaque while governing one bounded Module and Contract Change without whole-repository modeling.

exitCriteria: Adoption reports missing governance as Knowledge Gaps instead of inventing completeness.
An app, apk, and relay reference repository declares exact ownership and public Contracts while unrelated internals remain explicitly provisional or opaque.
One bounded Change reaches an Accepted Change Package without loading dependency implementation internals unless a recorded Context Expansion recomputes impact.

nonGoals: Automatically inferring a complete architecture
Requiring browser onboarding

outcomeRevisions.rationale: Replace the Context Expansion-dependent legacy exit list with stable materialized-reference, ordinary Kernel lifecycle, and existing CLI inspection Criteria before activation; later owner-scoped Changes must declare proof Claims, fixed routes, and implementation without claiming Worker read enforcement.

outcomeTransitions.rationale: Prior sealed Packages now cover every frozen LGT-010 Criterion and atomically close its Architecture Profile Gap plus every declared blocker without using this Plan Amendment as proof.

authorityAssessment.rationale: Prior Project Model implementation Packages directly cover validation, frozen Plan governance, and exact model-bound architecture compilation through project-model-references-are-consistent.
authorityAssessment.residualUncertainty: Planned Outcome refinement remains unsupported and is tracked separately; it does not weaken the frozen active LGT-010 Plan facts.

authorityAssessment.rationale: The accepted Kernel planRefs adapter Package directly proves that missing, unknown, non-active, or lossy Outcome references fail closed before Change compilation.
authorityAssessment.residualUncertainty: Browser Plan option requirements are not yet a canonical Workbench input projection, while Kernel enforcement remains authoritative and fail-closed.

authorityAssessment.rationale: The accepted Outcome Contribution compiler Package directly binds only selected Outcomes into the generated Context Capsule and exact verification subject.
authorityAssessment.residualUncertainty: Worker Context Expansion enforcement belongs to later Worker Execution Outcomes and is outside this Criterion.

authorityAssessment.rationale: Accepted compiler and Kernel Packages derive and revalidate exact content-addressed Contributions from the primary Module and Contract-visible Claims, with Plan-authority non-progress exceptions isolated.
authorityAssessment.residualUncertainty: A Contribution proves relevance rather than Criterion satisfaction; this Transition supplies the separate Plan-authority assessment required for satisfaction.

authorityAssessment.rationale: Prior sealed Transition compiler, Kernel lifecycle, and exact-package Packages bind status changes to Candidate-frozen history, Claim Evidence, Gap dispositions, and Plan authority at compile, Gate, and acceptance boundaries.
authorityAssessment.residualUncertainty: Accepted history remains local and unauthenticated across clones; the accepted-history-not-portable Gap remains open.

authorityAssessment.rationale: Project Model, Kernel, and Local Workbench Packages now project exact Outcome-to-Claim-to-Gate relations, current and sealed historical Evidence currency, Residual Uncertainty, and Knowledge Gaps through bounded source-bound views without aggregate assurance.
authorityAssessment.residualUncertainty: Browser DOM interaction and canonical Plan or acceptance-input choices remain explicit LGT-014-era Gaps; the Profile and Workbench semantic seams themselves are directly proved.

gapDispositions.rationale: The three declared Profile Closure Claims are covered by exact prior Project Model, Kernel, and Local Workbench Packages.
The Kernel and Local Workbench semantic projection Claims are covered after all declared blocker Gaps close in the same Transition.
The prior blocker-enforcement Package directly proves append-only validation, transitive derivation, route blocking, and atomic Closure consumption.
Prior Project Model and Kernel adoption Packages directly prove one bounded batch Claim and Gate projection with no consumer-side semantic traversal.

changeRules.planBeforeImplementation: A new or materially changed Outcome uses a plan-amendment Change that can touch only .legatura/** and cannot reference the plan it edits; implementation follows in a later Change against the accepted baseline.
changeRules.stableIds: Outcome ids are never reused or renamed; retired Outcomes remain as historical records with reasons and replacements.
changeRules.activation: Only Outcomes with satisfied dependencies may become active. Conditional Outcomes also require the activation condition in their acceptance criteria to be evidenced.
changeRules.refinement: A planned or conditional Outcome Definition changes only through an Accepted append-only Revision in an earlier Plan Amendment; an Amendment that appends any Revision cannot append any Outcome status Transition, and activation freezes the latest frozen-baseline Definition digest.
changeRules.contribution: An ordinary Change explicitly selects its Module, Outcome, and Claims. The compiler may infer a Criterion only from one unique exact Claim match; ambiguity requires a narrow Criterion hint and no match fails closed unless Plan authority records a non-progress exception.
changeRules.completion: Normative rule: an active Outcome becomes achieved only through a separate plan-amendment whose append-only Transition selects prior sealed Accepted Packages, supplies a Plan-authority assessment for every frozen Criterion, and disposes every referenced Knowledge Gap. The Candidate-frozen catalog forbids current-Change and post-Candidate proof; the Kernel re-resolves exact Package, Contribution, Gate Evidence, and ledger bindings before Gates and acceptance.
changeRules.frozenBaseline: A Change is compiled and accepted against its frozen Governance Baseline; editing the plan during that Change cannot authorize it.
changeRules.implementationTracking: Issues, pull requests, files, task plans, and agent conversations reference Outcomes but never become the Development Plan.

excluded.reason: Operational fault collection and attack replay are separate adapters. Reconsider only after Execution Record, Artifact Ledger, and replay semantics are stable; it is not a core S4 dependency.

excluded.capability: Agent marketplace and broad provider matrix
excluded.reason: Adapters prove a stable Worker Execution seam; adapter count is not a product outcome.

excluded.capability: Generic CI, cloud hosting, and model ranking
excluded.reason: These do not establish the local trusted-change architecture and remain external integrations.
